title: Use address() as sentinel to signal value overflow into a separate not(slot) storage location
trigger_patterns: value too large for packed slot
description: When packing a value into fewer bits than uint256, values that exceed the bit width need special handling. Using address() (20 bytes = 160 bits) as a sentinel in the packed field signals that the real value is stored at not(slot) — the bitwise NOT of the slot, which is deterministic and never collides with keccak256-derived slots.
when_to_apply: Packed storage where values occasionally exceed the packed bit width but usually fit
when_not_to_apply: Values that regularly exceed bit width (always two slots = simpler), mutable storage (not(slot) pattern is read-only friendly but complex to update)
